title: Shared group finance assumptions (letter-tier-agnostic)

documentation.exemplar: アキシブproject-style slice: seven members for salary and tokutenkai math (not necessarily full roster in database/groups.json).
documentation.legacy_typical_tier_d_filename: typical_regular_live.json → typical_tier_d_group.json (was typical_rank_d_group.json; scenario title is Typical Tier D group)
documentation.scenario_filenames: Letter-tier examples: typical_tier_b_group.json, typical_tier_c_group.json (Typical Tier C), typical_tier_d_group.json (Typical Tier D).
documentation.monthly_live_counts_matrix: Editable grid: `database/finance/monthly_live_counts_by_letter_tier_template.csv` — rows **letter tiers S–F**, columns **type_1 … type_7** (average monthly occurrences) plus **`type_6_venue_rank`** (venue **rank** letter for type **6** one-mans in that tier row, for `venue_reference.json`). **Type S** overlay cadence is roster-derived, not a column; see `type_S_overlay_monthly_rule`.
documentation.type_S_overlay_monthly_rule: Live **type S** is a **special overlay** on a **host** type (4 / 6 / 7 / …), not its own venue cadence. **Average monthly S occurrences** = `member_count × S_events_per_member_per_year ÷ 12` (e.g. one birthday live per member per year on the host format → divide by 12 for a monthly rate). `derived_monthly_event_counts` in `typical_tier_*_group.json` may expose this as `type_S_on_6_birthday`, `type_S_on_7_birthday`, etc.; host choice is tier/scenario-specific.
documentation.commercial_income_guess_s_through_b: Rough **monthly** CM / endorsement **gross** and **group-net strip** after agencies keep the bulk: `commercial_income_guess_letter_tier_s_through_b` (tiers **S, A, B** only).
documentation.cd_sales_model: **Physical CD** net per unit and bundled **online signing** labor; **digital sales** and **MV / streaming watch income** are out of scope for that block—see `cd_sales_model` root key.
documentation.member_compensation_by_letter_tier: Base salary multiplier and tokutenkai sales bonus rate by letter tier (S..F), including E part-time (1/3 base) and F no-base + 10% tokutenkai bonus; tier-F also has no hair/make budget; see root `member_compensation_by_letter_tier`.

venue_assumptions_by_live_type.type_7_routine_live: Baseline follows **group letter tier**: tier D → livehouse_club; tier C → hall_medium; larger tiers per venue_reference.json (venue **rank** letters are room-scale and separate).
venue_assumptions_by_live_type.type_S_on_7_birthday: Often hosted on type 7 (inherits host venue tier); tier-C touring template may host **S on type 6** instead—see `typical_tier_c_group.json`.

_formulas.type_6_one_man: 1 event every N months → 12/N events per year ÷ 12
_formulas.type_S_on_7_birthday: member_count × 1 birthday (S on host type 7) per year ÷ 12; member_count taken from type_7_routine_live_single_show.member_count in the tier-specific scenario file

staff_count_by_group_letter_tier._note: Touring / live-ops **staff headcount** (not on-stage members) vs **member_number** `n`. Code: `FinanceSystem.staff_count_for_group_letter_tier(n, letter_tier)`. For **n > 20**, only **S, A, B** use the second formula set; **C–F** keep the **n ≤ 20** expressions evaluated at the actual `n`.

when_n_lte_small_roster_max.F: max(2, floor(n / 5)) i.e. max(2, n // 5) — not in the original six-formula prose; tapered floor; fork if you change F.

commercial_income_guess_letter_tier_s_th._note: **Illustrative guess** — national / major CM, brand tie-ins, and similar **commercial** flows **per calendar month** (smooth average; real deals are lumpy). In practice **most gross is retained by each member’s agency** (and holding companies); the **group operating budget** often only sees **pooled unit deals, management fees, or thin pass-through**—modeled here as a **small share of gross**.

cd_sales_model._note: **Physical CD** unit economics to the **group** after manufacturing / distribution / channel cuts—**average net ¥1,500 per CD sold**. Each CD is bundled with an **online signing** obligation: **one allocating member spends 30 wall-clock seconds per CD** (serial meet load). **Digital sales** and **MV / streaming watch revenue** are **not** modeled here (intentionally ignored for this block). Helpers: `FinanceSystem.cd_sales_net_income_yen`, `FinanceSystem.cd_online_signing_member_seconds`.

online_signing_event._note: Per **sold CD**, the member handling that unit’s lane owes **30 s** of online signing time (not split across the whole group unless you fork the data model).

member_compensation_by_letter_tier._note: Compensation policy by group letter tier. Base salary uses `base_salary_multiplier_vs_default_monthly_base_salary` against the default anchor (`default_financial_constants` / scenario anchor). C through S receive higher base salary plus sales bonus; E is part-time at 1/3 base; F has no base salary, tokutenkai bonus is fixed at 10%, and hair/make budget is disabled.

F._note: Tier F does not budget hair&make for lives in this model.

member_compensation_by_letter_tier._policy_note: F tier overrides tokutenkai bonus to 0.1, base salary to 0, and makeup/hair to disabled. E tier base salary is part-time (1/3).

default_financial_constants._note: Defaults used by finance helpers unless overridden in a tier scenario file.

type_4_taiban_tokutenkai._note: Lane model for **type 4 (taiban)** post-live meet; aligned with `ui/main_ui.py` Taiban preset and `database/idol_group_rank_table.md` type 4. Type-7 routine templates live in `typical_tier_*_group.json` scenario files.
type_4_taiban_tokutenkai._blended_slot_note: At 50/50 ticket counts, mean time per ticket is (15+20)/2 = 17.5s (capacity uses n×15 + n×20 ≤ window per member).

live_goods_model._note: In-show / venue goods table (where offered): gross ≈ floor(effective_buyers × average_price_yen × average_buying_pieces). **Host types 4 (taiban), 5 (multi-man), and 7 (routine) do not offer goods** in this model—gross is always 0 for those hosts (including **S on 7**). Types **1, 2, 3, 6** (and **S on 6** etc.) may sell goods; **type S** overlay adds `type_S_buying_pieces_add` to pieces when the **host** type is goods-eligible. **Letter tiers** S..F set price and piece ladder. Implementations: `database/finance/finance_system.py` → `FinanceSystem.estimate_live_goods_gross_yen` / `live_goods_effective_buyers`.

effective_buyers_formula_by_host_live_ty._legend: Only **1, 2, 3, 6** use `group_fans` as effective buyers here. Types **4, 5, 7** are listed as **no goods**—see `no_goods_host_live_types`.

per_live_financial_table_template_note: Income/expense rows for each live type; per-occurrence nets align to event_net_per_occurrence_estimate_yen in typical_tier_*_group.json.

staff_salary.note: Producer is modeled as player-owner (no fixed salary in this baseline); two managers are paid monthly salary.

additional_cost_assumptions.costume_refresh_inference_note: Given tier D = ¥20,000 and tier B = ¥40,000 per member per quarter, inferred ladder is S 60k, A 50k, C 30k, E 15k, F 10k (B fixed at 40k).

makeup_hairstyle.birthday_special_case: For birthday live (type_S_on_7 in this model), apply styling to 1 member only.
makeup_hairstyle._note: Baseline block matches **letter tier D** (`typical_tier_d_group.json`). Letter **tier C and above** use `makeup_hairstyle_letter_tier_c_and_above` instead.

makeup_hairstyle_letter_tier_c_and_above._note: Budget hair/makeup for **every member** on **every** modeled live in types 3–7 and type S overlays. Scenario files `typical_tier_c_group.json` / `typical_tier_b_group.json` apply this to their live tables.

major_label_mv_cycle_letter_tier_c_and_a._note: Four **major-publisher**, MV-grade drops per year; per-release anchor matches `publishing_costs_yen.major_publisher` (¥1M) unless a scenario forks it.

song_and_music_production.note: Use lower end for simple arrangements/indie production and upper end for full studio package with richer arrangement and recording sessions.

publishing_costs_yen.note: Publishing/distribution onboarding and related release-side cost anchor.

monthly_admin_training_costs_per_tier_ye.inference_note: Anchored at letter tier D = admin ¥100,000 and training ¥200,000 per month; other letter tiers use the same multipliers as before (S3.0 / A2.5 / B2.0 / C1.5 / E0.7 / F0.5).
